vulnerability.reference: Copyright systems depend on watermarks as the sole barrier against unauthorized AI training on protected works.
vulnerability.generated: Artists rely on watermarking algorithms that are inherently susceptible to adversarial and diffusive attacks, undermining protection.

impact.reference: Artists lose revenue and recognition, discouraging creative production.
impact.generated: Artists suffer economic losses and loss of attribution as diffusion models train on their works without authorization or compensation.

dose_response.reference: Adversarial watermark forgery bypasses attribution, which in copyright enforcement exposes artists relying on watermarks, leading to revenue loss and market flooding with indistinguishable AI outputs.
dose_response.generated: Adversarial and diffusive attacks exploit watermark fragility, enabling forgery that defeats detection, through artists embedding watermarks for protection, triggers reliance on attack-susceptible algorithms undermining protection, leading to economic losses and lost attribution as diffusion models train on their works without authorization.
